antipattern.symptom: Production `RailsConfig.from_content(...)` with hard-coded YAML + Colang strings; policy lives in `.py` files alongside business logic.
antipattern.root-cause: Test-only shortcut promoted to production without refactor.
antipattern.fix: Move config to `config/` directory; load via `RailsConfig.from_path("./config")`. Inline form lives only under `tests/`.

antipattern.symptom: Intent collapses — user asks "show my order" but bot does not match "ask about order status" intent because only one variant was provided.
antipattern.root-cause: Single-example intents have no embedding spread; near-paraphrases fall outside the matching radius.
antipattern.fix: Each `define user X` block carries ≥3 examples covering paraphrase, command, question forms. Add a smoke test that hits each variant.

antipattern.symptom: Throughput collapses under load — one synchronous PII action blocks the asyncio loop, all parallel conversations stall behind it.
antipattern.root-cause: Action declared `def` instead of `async def`; blocking I/O inside the loop.
antipattern.fix: Convert every `@action()` to `async def`; wrap CPU-bound work in `asyncio.to_thread`. Smoke test with concurrent conversations.

antipattern.symptom: RAG-backed support bot hallucinates product details that aren't in the KB; users see wrong refund policy.
antipattern.root-cause: Output rails miss `check facts`; bot answers from prior-prompt context without grounding.
antipattern.fix: Add `check facts` to `rails.output.flows`; ensure `knowledge_base:` block declares the local docs path; register custom `check_facts` action that returns False unless ≥1 supporting document found.
